title: Single Sub-Dollar Digital Goods Authorization Test with Disposable Email
narrative: Fraudster validates a stolen card by making a single small purchase ($0.50-$1.99) at a digital goods merchant (ProductCD='W' or 'C') using a disposable email domain (P_emaildomain in ['gmail.com','yahoo.com','outlook.com'] is low risk, but 'guerrillamail.com','tempmail.net','10minutemail.com' raises flags). The transaction occurs outside business hours (txn_hour between 23-05 or is_night=1) to avoid immediate detection. Card shows card1_txn_count_1day=1 and card1_txn_count_7day=1, indicating first observed use. The billing and shipping addresses often mismatch significantly (dist1 > 5000 km or addr1 differs entirely). TransactionAmt clusters tightly around $0.99 or $1.50â€”just above minimum authorization thresholds but below fraud review triggers at many issuers. No CVV decline (transaction_result='approved'), but AVS may show partial mismatch. Within 2-6 hours, if test succeeds, fraudster pivots to high-value purchases elsewhere using same card.
indicators: TransactionAmt between $0.50 and $1.99 at digital goods merchant (ProductCD='W' or 'C')
P_emaildomain matches known disposable email providers (guerrillamail.com, tempmail.net, 10minutemail.com, etc.)
card1_txn_count_1day=1 and card1_txn_count_7day=1 indicating first card appearance
Transaction occurs during off-hours: txn_hour between 23-05 or is_night=1
dist1 > 5000 km or billing/shipping addr1 fields show significant mismatch
card1_amt_zscore near 0 for amount ($0.99 or $1.50 common test values)
Approved transaction (no CVV decline) but AVS shows partial address mismatch
summary: A first-time card makes a single sub-$2 purchase at a digital goods merchant during overnight hours, using a disposable email address and showing a large distance between billing and shipping locations.
reasoning: Fraudsters test stolen cards with tiny charges at low-scrutiny merchants to confirm the card works before attempting larger fraudulent purchases elsewhere within hours.
indicator_explanations: Tiny purchase at digital store tests if card works without triggering alerts
Email is from a throwaway service used once and discarded
Card appears for the very first time in system history
Transaction happens in the middle of the night when monitoring is lighter
Billing and shipping locations are impossibly far apart or deeply inconsistent
Amount matches classic test values fraudsters use to verify card validity
Card approved despite address verification showing cardholder location doesn't match
